content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

May 15, 1990

Lance Olson  
Olson, Connelly, Hagel,  
Fong & Leidigh  
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350  
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice  
Our File No. A-90-204

Dear Mr. Olson:

You have requested confirmation of advice provided to you concerning permissible expenditures of campaign funds. You requested this advice on behalf of Senator John Garamendi.

This letter confirms my advice that, under the Political Reform Act,1 Senator Garamendi may expend campaign funds (1) to purchase additional automobile insurance for a state-leased vehicle used for legislative and governmental purposes, and (2) baby sitting services on specific occasions when the Senator and his wife are not available to care for their minor children due to attendance at campaign related activities, such as fundraisers. We have concluded that, in both instances, the expenditures in question are directly related to a political, legislative or governmental purpose, as required by Sections 85801 and 85804.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan  
General Counsel

KED:plh

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
